chapterSummary.title: Why? / Alice in Wonderland
chapterSummary.content: This unit brings together a poem, a story, and a grammar lesson, all connected by the theme of curiosity and asking questions. The poem 'Why?' celebrates a child's natural habit of questioning everything in the world around them. The story 'Alice in Wonderland' takes students into a magical land where a curious girl named Alice has amazing adventures after following a White Rabbit down a rabbit hole. The grammar section teaches question words (Wh-words) such as What, Where, When, Who, Why, Which, and How, and shows how these words help us ask clear and meaningful questions.
keyPoints: The poem 'Why?' encourages curiosity and the habit of asking questions
Alice in Wonderland is a story about a curious girl who discovers a magical land
Alice follows a White Rabbit down a rabbit hole and has surprising adventures
Question words (What, Where, When, Who, Why, Which, How) help us ask different kinds of questions
Every question sentence must end with a question mark (?)
learningObjectives: Understand the theme of curiosity in the poem 'Why?'
Retell the key events from the story 'Alice in Wonderland'
Identify and describe the main characters in the story
Use the correct question word (What, Where, When, Who, Why, Which, How) to form questions
Punctuate questions correctly with a question mark

curatedNotes.title: Question Word Quick Guide
curatedNotes.content: Remember which question word to use: Who = person, What = thing or action, Where = place, When = time, Why = reason, Which = choice from options, How = manner or amount. Try making one question with each word every day to practise!

curatedNotes.title: Alice in Wonderland -- Key Events
curatedNotes.content: Remember the story sequence: (1) Alice sits by the riverbank. (2) She sees a White Rabbit in a coat with a watch. (3) She follows the Rabbit out of curiosity. (4) She falls down a deep rabbit hole. (5) She arrives in Wonderland. (6) She drinks from a bottle and grows tall. (7) She eats a cake and becomes tiny. These events show how curiosity leads to adventure!

curatedNotes.title: Curiosity Activity
curatedNotes.content: Be like the child in the poem 'Why?' -- pick one thing you see today (a cloud, a plant, a shadow) and write down three questions about it using different question words. For example: What is a shadow? Why does it change shape? When does it disappear? Share your questions with a friend or family member!
